qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Pre-auth OpenWire frame length triggers heap allocation and broker denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an OpenWire parser availability bug in OpenWireFrameParser.decode on the OPENWIRE listener, while the advisory is a missing-authentication flaw in the Core federation control path that can force outbound rogue-broker federation connections for message injection or exfiltration.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Core federation authentication flaw or equivalent vulnerable control flow. The session's only final qualifying finding is a distinct pre-auth OpenWire heap-allocation denial of service.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes an unauthenticated attacker using the Core protocol to force the broker to establish an outbound Core federation connection to an attacker-controlled rogue broker, enabling message injection or exfiltration.

The only final qualifying finding in .judge/input_manifest.json and openwire_pre_auth_heap_allocation_dos/vulnerability_report.json is a pre-auth OpenWire frame-length allocation bug in OpenWireFrameParser.decode that causes OutOfMemoryError or availability loss.

artifacts/component_family_checklist.md separately notes the outbound broker connections and federation family as pruned rather than accepted, so no final qualifying finding covers the advisory's Core federation root cause.
